title: Citi Raises Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) Price Target to $55
content:
Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP) is one of the MLP Stocks List: 20 Largest MLPs.Citi Raises Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) Price Target to $55

On April 2, 2026, Citi raised its price target on Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP) from $49 to $55. Despite the positive price target adjustment, the firm’s analyst Spiro Dounis maintained the Sell rating on the stock. According to the analyst’s research note, the conflict in the Middle East could have an enduring effect on the global market that benefits U.S. liquefied natural gas in the long-term.

Separately, on March 27, 2026, JPMorgan raised its price target on Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP) from $57 to $63 while maintaining an Underweight rating on the stock. The firm adjusted Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP)’s financial model to incorporate the current strip pricing.

Prior to this, on March 26, 2026, UBS also raised its price target on Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP) by $13, from $62 to $75. The analyst maintains a Neutral rating on the company’s stock.

Founded in 2006, Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE:CQP) is an international energy company with headquarters in Texas that provides liquefied natural gas (LNG) to integrated energy companies, utilities, and energy trading companies around the world through its subsidiaries.

While we acknowledge the potential of CQP as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: Does Leadership Consolidation at Cheniere Energy (LNG) Reframe the Governance Pillar of Its Investment Story?
content:
Cheniere Energy has announced that longtime chairman G. Andrea Botta will retire at the May 14, 2026 annual meeting, after 16 years on the board and 10 years as chairman, with current President and CEO Jack Fusco set to assume the combined role of chairman, president and CEO, while Patricia Collawn becomes lead director to reinforce independent oversight. This shift to a combined chairman-CEO structure, supported by a lead independent director, marks a governance transition that could influence how investors assess Cheniere’s leadership continuity and board independence. We’ll now examine how consolidating the chairman and CEO roles under Jack Fusco may shape Cheniere Energy’s existing investment narrative.

We've uncovered the 12 dividend fortresses yielding 5%+ that don't just survive market storms, but thrive in them.

Cheniere Energy Investment Narrative Recap

To stay invested in Cheniere, you need to believe that global LNG demand and long term contracts can continue to support its large, capital intensive export platform, despite policy and oversupply risks. The move to combine the chairman and CEO roles under Jack Fusco, offset by appointing a lead independent director, appears more like a governance evolution than a change that materially alters the key near term catalyst of upcoming quarterly results or the biggest risk around long term LNG market balance.

Among recent announcements, the upcoming Q1 2026 earnings release on May 7 stands out, as it will be the first major checkpoint for investors assessing how Cheniere is balancing heavy capex, high leverage and shareholder returns after another year of strong profitability. In this context, the governance shift around the May 14 AGM sits in the background, while the earnings print and any commentary on demand, contract stability and expansion plans are likely to matter more for the existing thesis.

However, investors should also be aware that if global LNG capacity additions push the market into prolonged oversupply, Cheniere’s ability to sustain its current margin profile and capital return program could...

Read the full narrative on Cheniere Energy (it's free!)

Cheniere Energy’s narrative projects $23.9 billion revenue and $2.9 billion earnings by 2029. This requires 7.0% yearly revenue growth and an earnings decrease of $2.4 billion from $5.3 billion today.

Uncover how Cheniere Energy's forecasts yield a $295.05 fair value, a 7% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other PerspectivesLNG 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Six fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community cluster between US$295 and US$393, showing how far apart individual views on Cheniere’s worth can be. Against that backdrop, concerns about a potential global LNG oversupply and its impact on pricing, contract resilience and long term earnings mean you are weighing a wide spread of expectations against one of the central risks to the business.

title: Assessing Cheniere Energy (LNG) Valuation After A Strong Multi Year Shareholder Return Run
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

What Cheniere Energy’s recent performance means for investors

Cheniere Energy (LNG) has drawn fresh attention after a strong past 3 months, with the stock showing a 40.06% return and a 39.14% gain year to date, prompting investors to reassess expectations.

See our latest analysis for Cheniere Energy.

In the short term, the share price has cooled from recent highs with a 3.19% one day decline. However, the 30 day share price return of 9.73% and 90 day gain of 40.06% suggest momentum has been building, supported by a 27.55% one year total shareholder return and an 85.32% three year total shareholder return.

If you are looking beyond Cheniere to other energy linked ideas, it could be a good moment to scan the nuclear infrastructure space via our 93 nuclear energy infrastructure stocks

With Cheniere trading at US$275.21, sitting about 9.5% below one set of analyst targets and carrying an implied 30% intrinsic discount, the key question is simple: is there still a buying opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 14.2% Undervalued

Cheniere Energy’s most followed narrative, according to superbullll, places fair value at $320.94 per share versus the last close of $275.21. This comparison frames the recent price strength in a different light.

This narrative is being written during one of the most consequential energy disruptions since 2022. Following US and Israeli strikes on Iran beginning February 28, 2026, the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz a closed military zone. Iranian retaliatory strikes damaged QatarEnergy's facilities at Ras Laffan, forcing Qatar to declare force majeure on LNG shipments, removing roughly 20% of global LNG supply from the market almost overnight. European gas prices spiked approximately 20% on March 2 alone, with TTF closing at €54/MWh versus €32/MWh the prior Friday.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see how one investor gets from today’s share price to that higher fair value? The narrative leans heavily on contracted cash flows, expansion capacity and profitability assumptions that, according to this view, put future earnings power well beyond current market expectations.

Result: Fair Value of $320.94 (UNDERVALUED)

However, investors still need to weigh risks such as a faster than expected easing of the Hormuz disruption or shifts in US LNG export policy that could pressure sentiment.

title: Cheniere Announces Timing of First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
content:
HOUSTON, April 09, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Cheniere Energy, Inc. ("Cheniere") (NYSE: LNG) announced today that it plans to issue its earnings release with respect to first quarter 2026 financial results on Thursday, May 7, 2026 before the market opens. Cheniere will host a conference call for investors and analysts at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time (10:00 a.m. Central Time) that day to discuss first quarter results.

A listen-only webcast of the call and accompanying slide presentation will be available at www.cheniere.com. After completion of the webcast, a replay will be available on the Cheniere website.

About Cheniere

Cheniere Energy, Inc. is the leading producer and exporter of liquefied natural gas ("LNG") in the United States, reliably providing a clean, secure, and affordable solution to the growing global need for natural gas. Cheniere is a full-service LNG provider, with capabilities that include gas procurement and transportation, liquefaction, vessel chartering, and LNG delivery. Cheniere has one of the largest liquefaction platforms in the world, consisting of the Sabine Pass liquefaction facility, wholly-owned by its subsidiary Cheniere Energy Partners, L.P. (NYSE: CQP), and the Corpus Christi liquefaction facility, each on the U.S. Gulf Coast with total combined production capacity of over 53 million tonnes per annum ("mtpa") of LNG in operation and an additional approximately 8 mtpa of expected production capacity under construction. Cheniere is also pursuing liquefaction expansion opportunities and other projects along the LNG value chain. Cheniere is headquartered in Houston, Texas, and has additional offices in London, Singapore, Beijing, Tokyo, Dubai and Washington, D.C.

For additional information, please refer to the Cheniere website at www.cheniere.com and Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains certain statements that may include "forward-looking statements" within the meanings of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. All statements, other than statements of historical or present facts or conditions, included herein are "forward-looking statements." Included among "forward-looking statements" are, among other things, (i) statements regarding Cheniere’s financial and operational guidance, business strategy, plans and objectives, including the development, construction and operation of liquefaction facilities, (ii) statements regarding regulatory authorization and approval expectations, (iii) statements expressing beliefs and expectations regarding the development of Cheniere’s LNG terminal and pipeline businesses, including liquefaction facilities, (iv) statements regarding the business operations and prospects of third-parties, (v) statements regarding potential financing arrangements, (vi) statements regarding future discussions and entry into contracts, (vii) statements relating to Cheniere’s capital deployment, including intent, ability, extent, and timing of capital expenditures, debt repayment, dividends, share repurchases and execution on the capital allocation plan and (viii) statements relating to our goals, commitments and strategies in relation to environmental matters. Although Cheniere believes that the expectations reflected in these forward-looking statements are reasonable, they do involve assumptions, risks and uncertainties, and these expectations may prove to be incorrect. Cheniere’s actual results could differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements as a result of a variety of factors, including those discussed in Cheniere’s periodic reports that are filed with and available from the Securities and Exchange Commission. You should not place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date of this press release. Other than as required under the securities laws, Cheniere does not assume a duty to update these forward-looking statements.

content:
Energy stocks are getting hammered after the U.S. agreed to a two-week truce with Iran, conditioned on the Strait of Hormuz reopening immediately. In premarket trading in the U.S.: Liquefied natural gas exporter Venture Global (VG) tumbled more than 10%.

title: This is How Cheniere Energy Benefits From Tight LNG Supply
content:
Cheniere Energy LNG is supported by a tightening global liquefied natural gas (“LNG”) market, where ongoing supply disruptions are limiting exports and slowing new capacity additions. Issues in key regions like Qatar, along with continued geopolitical tensions involving Russia and the Middle East, have reduced available supply. This has created a stronger pricing environment. Europe, still heavily dependent on imported LNG, is expected to remain a steady buyer, supporting long-term demand for U.S. exports.

Cheniere Energy’s recent performance reflects these favorable conditions. In 2025, revenues increased 27% and net income rose 64%, even though LNG volumes saw only modest growth. Higher realized prices and better cost control were the main drivers. Cheniere is also expanding its capacity through projects like Corpus Christi Stage 3, while maintaining a strong pipeline of future developments. This gives it clear visibility into long-term growth tied to global LNG demand.

Financially, Cheniere Energy is in a stronger position as its balance sheet also improved in 2025. Net interest expense fell to $948 million (or 4.7% of revenues) from $1 billion (6.4% of revenues) in 2024, while long-term debt remained stable at $22.5 billion. Lower financing costs improve the company’s ability to support future expansion projects. At the same time, disciplined execution has helped strengthen cash flows. However, with the stock already seeing a strong run-up in recent months, much of the near-term upside from higher LNG prices may be reflected in its valuation. Even so, tight global supply and steady demand should continue to support earnings going forward.

While Cheniere Energy remains a key beneficiary of this tightening market, it is not the only company positioned to gain. Several other LNG-focused players are also well placed to benefit from the current supply crunch and sustained global demand.

Other LNG Stocks to Focus on Amid Global Supply Crunch

Venture Global VG: It is a U.S.-based producer and exporter focused on delivering low-cost LNG to global markets. Venture Global operates a vertically integrated model spanning production, transport, shipping and regasification, with key projects along the Gulf Coast. With capacity exceeding 100 million tons per annum across operating, construction and development stages, Venture Global is rapidly scaling its presence in the LNG market. Its modular approach sets it apart. Pre-built liquefaction units are assembled offsite and installed quickly, improving safety, lowering costs and reducing execution risks. This model supports faster project timelines and consistent delivery, helping expand access to cleaner energy worldwide.

Chevron CVX: This is another world-class operator of LNG. The giant Gorgon and Wheatstone developments in Australia are part of Chevron’s long-term strategy and are its flagship LNG developments. These mega projects allow the supermajor to tap the strong Asian LNG demand. Combined, these projects have an annual LNG production capacity exceeding 24 million metric tons. Chevron is the operator of both projects, with a stake of 64.14% in Wheatstone and 47.3% in the Gorgon development.

Story Continues

title: Is It Time To Revisit Cheniere Energy (LNG) After Its Strong Multi Year Run?
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Wondering if Cheniere Energy's current share price reflects its true worth, or if the market is leaving some value on the table. The stock has seen a mixed short term picture with a 3.3% decline over the last 7 days, an 11.3% gain over 30 days, and returns of 43.6% year to date and 41.8% over 1 year. These moves sit against a longer backdrop where the share price return is 90.7% over 3 years and around 3x over 5 years. This gives helpful context for anyone thinking about entry points or position sizing today. This article was triggered to provide evergreen coverage, so the focus is less on a single headline and more on how the current price stacks up against a range of valuation checks. Right now Cheniere Energy carries a valuation score of 5/6. The sections that follow will break down what that means across different valuation methods and point to a more complete way to think about value at the end of the article.

Find out why Cheniere Energy's 41.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: Cheniere Energy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes a series of future cash flow estimates and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return, aiming to express what those future $ cash flows are worth in present terms.

For Cheniere Energy, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $2.9b. Analyst based projections extend out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year modeled at $3.5b, and further years are extrapolated by Simply Wall St using gradually moderating growth assumptions.

Pulling those projections together, the DCF output suggests an intrinsic value of $392.89 per share. Compared with the current share price, this implies an intrinsic discount of 27.7%, which indicates the shares are trading below this model’s estimate of fair value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Cheniere Energy is undervalued by 27.7%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks.LNG Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Cheniere Energy.

Approach 2: Cheniere Energy Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that support it. It gives a quick sense of how much the market is willing to pay for every dollar of profit.

Story Continues

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E often reflects two things: how quickly earnings are expected to grow, and how risky those earnings are. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower one.

Cheniere Energy currently trades on a P/E of 11.23x. That sits below the Oil and Gas industry average P/E of 15.56x and below the broader peer group average of 20.02x.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Cheniere Energy is 17.84x. This is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might be appropriate given factors like the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and key risks. Because it blends these company specific inputs, the Fair Ratio can give a more tailored gauge than a simple comparison with peers or an industry average.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 17.84x with the current P/E of 11.23x suggests the shares trade below this metric’s indication of fair value.

Result: UNDERVALUEDNYSE:LNG P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Cheniere Energy Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, and Narratives are that tool, letting you turn your view of Cheniere Energy into a clear story that connects the business, a forecast and a fair value, then compares that fair value with the current price so you can judge whether the stock fits your plan today.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit within the Community page and work like a guided framework, where you set assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins, and the platform converts that story into a pricing model that updates as new data like earnings or news arrives.

For Cheniere, one published Narrative currently applies inputs that lead to a fair value of US$320.94 per share, while another uses a different set of assumptions and arrives at US$295.05, illustrating how two investors can look at the same company, weigh its LNG contracts, expansion projects and risks differently, and still translate those views into concrete numbers that are easy to compare with the live share price.

Do you think there's more to the story for Cheniere Energy? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!NYSE:LNG 1-Year Stock Price Chart

title: Top 10 large-cap energy stocks ranked by their forward dividend yield
content:
[Working Pumpjacks On Sunset]
imaginima/E+ via Getty Images

As market grapples with volatility, below is a list of the top 10 large-cap energy stocks with market capitalizations of $10 billion or more, ranked by their forward dividend yield.

The list is topped by Western Midstream Partners, LP (WES [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WES]), with a dividend yield of 8.84%. MPLX LP (MPLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MPLX]) and Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. (PAA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PAA]) are next, with Energy Transfer LP (ET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ET]) and Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (EPD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPD]) rounding out the rest of the top five.

title: Cheniere Energy Inc (LNG) Sees Huge LNG Growth in Asia as Qatar Struggles
content:
Cheniere Energy Inc (NYSE:LNG) is among the must-buy non-tech stocks to invest in now. On March 27, Reuters reported that Cheniere Energy’s fifth processing unit at the Corpus Christi LNG ( liquefied natural gas) plant in Texas had begun operating at full capacity.Cheniere Energy Inc (LNG) Sees Huge LNG Growth in Asia as Qatar Struggles

Cheniere has been working to expand its Corpus Christi facility with seven additional processing units to boost its LNG export capacity by 10 million metric tons per year. According to the report, the new processing unit, called Train 5, will boost ​output by around 1.5 million tons a year. Cheniere is working to complete the remaining ​two units in the Corpus Christi expansion project.

Texas-based Cheniere is America’s largest LNG exporter. According to Reuters, the attacks on Qatar’s LNG facilities during the Iran war has affected the global supply of LNG, considering that Qatar is the world’s largest producer of LNG. This has resulted in shortages in Asia, and so Cheniere is looking to send more cargoes to that region, according to the report. It may take up to five years for QatarEnergy to restore its LNG output lost due to the attacks on its facilities.

In a separate Reuters report on March 25, Cheniere CEO Jack Fusco said he expected LNG growth to come from Asia, even though Europe remained the market of choice. According to the executive, Cheniere has sent 1,600 cargoes to Europe since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022.

On March 24, Wolfe Research lifted its price target on Cheniere stock to $315 from $270 while reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. According to the equity research firm, the Iran war reinvigorates the growth story for Cheniere.

Cheniere Energy Inc (NYSE:LNG) is an American energy company focused on the liquefied natural gas (LNG) business. The company owns and operates liquefied natural gas production, storage, and transport facilities. With major facilities in Louisiana and Texas, Cheniere primarily serves international markets.

While we acknowledge the potential of LNG as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.
